text.title: backup-filesystem-restic Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for backup-filesystem-restic as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent commits RESTIC_PASSWORD or BORG_PASSPHRASE literal into a backup script or .env file tracked by Git.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Encryption key loss = data loss; key exposure in Git = security incident. Always load from secrets manager. Add pre-commit check: `git diff --staged | grep -E 'RESTIC_PASSWORD|BORG_PASSPHRASE'`.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates `restic forget` without `--prune`, then reports the repository is cleaned up while data packs still consume full storage.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: forget removes snapshot references but does not reclaim storage. --prune must run to garbage-collect unreferenced packs. Use `forget --prune` to do both in one pass.

failure-mode.description: Agent adds `restic forget --prune` as a guardrail-free operation that an autonomous agent can trigger without human approval.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Forget/prune is irreversible. Agent may emit `forget --dry-run` only; a human runs the real prune. Guard this operation explicitly.
